segments.speaker: MAN
segments.en: And does that include breakfast?

segments.speaker: MAN
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的。那如果我们继续推进这件事，你能告诉我一些关于费用……的信息吗？

segments.speaker: MAN
segments.en: So if we go ahead with this, can you give me some information about how much ...

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Good morning.
segments.zh:
早上好。我叫露西·克里滕登，是一家在本地区组织志愿活动的机构的运营总监。我希望我能说服你们中的一两位成为志愿者。让我先简要解释一下我们所说的志愿服务是什么意思。

志愿者是指那些选择花一些时间，无偿地以某种方式帮助他人的青少年和成年人。大多数志愿者每周投入两三个小时，而少数人则做得更多。他们帮助的人可能有身体或行为上的困难，例如。

志愿者可以做各种各样的事情，取决于他们自己的能力和兴趣。例如，如果他们支持一个陷入困境的家庭，他们可能会在烹饪上给出建议，或者推荐如何规划预算，或者如何用他们的收入明智地购物。他们甚至可能做一些油漆或贴墙纸的工作，也许和家庭中能够做这些事的成员一起。或者甚至做一些保姆工作，以便父母可以出去一会儿。

志愿服务的好处不仅仅在于被帮助的人。志愿者也从中获益：他们利用自己的技能来应对某人的精神或身体健康问题，志愿服务可能是他们求职简历上的宝贵元素：雇主通常对愿意花时间帮助他人的人有好感。重要的是，大多数志愿者觉得他们所做的工作给了他们生活的目标。在我看来，他们在这一点上很幸运，因为很多人没有这种感觉。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: My name's Lucy Crittenden, and I'm the Director of Operations for an organisation that arranges volunteering in this part of the country.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I'm hoping I can persuade one or two of you to become volunteers yourselves.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Let me start by briefly explaining what we mean by volunteering.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Volunteers are teenagers and adults who choose to spend some time, unpaid, helping other people in some way.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Most volunteers devote two or three hours to this every week, while a few do much more.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The people they help may have physical or behavioural difficulties, for example.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Volunteers can do all sorts of things, depending on their own abilities and interests.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: If they're supporting a family that's struggling, for example, they may be able to give them tips on cooking, or recommend how to plan their budget or how to shop sensibly on their income.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They might even do some painting or wallpapering, perhaps alongside any members of the family who are able to do it.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Or even do some babysitting so that parents can go out for a while.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The benefit from volunteering isn't only for the people being helped.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Volunteers also gain from it: they're using their skills to cope with somebody's mental or physical ill health, and volunteering may be a valuable element of their CV when they're applying for jobs: employers usually look favourably on someone who's given up time to help others.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Significantly, most volunteers feel that what they're doing gives them a purpose in their lives.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: And in my opinion, they're lucky in that respect, as many people don't have that feeling.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Now I'd like to tell you what some of our volunteers have said about what they do, to give you an idea of the range of ways in which they can help people.
segments.zh:
现在我想告诉你们我们的一些志愿者对他们所做工作的评价，让你们了解他们帮助他人的各种方式。

哈比卜支持一位开始出现痴呆迹象的老太太。每周一次，他们和其他老年人一起去当地的社区中心，那里有一群人进来唱歌。这些歌曲把听众带回到他们的青年时代，让他们暂时忘记现在面临的困难。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Habib supports an elderly lady who's beginning to show signs of dementia.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Once a week they, along with other elderly people, go to the local community centre, where a group of people come in and sing.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The songs take the listeners back to their youth, and for a little while they can forget the difficulties that they face now.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Our volunteer Consuela is an amazing woman.
segments.zh: 我们的志愿者康苏埃拉是一位了不起的女性。她自己走路有困难，但她没有让这阻止她。她帮助了几个有类似困难、几乎完全停止走路的人。通过以身作则，康苏埃拉鼓励他们走得越来越多。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: She has difficulty walking herself, but she doesn't let that stop her.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: She helps a couple of people with similar difficulties, who had almost stopped walking altogether.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: By using herself as an example, Consuela encourages them to walk more and more.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Minh visits a young man who lives alone and can't leave his home on his own, so he hardly ever saw anyone.
segments.zh: 明拜访一位独居的年轻人，他不能独自离开家，所以几乎见不到任何人。但他们一起去看电影，或者去看那个年轻人很久没能拜访的朋友。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But together they go out to the cinema, or to see friends the young man hadn't been able to visit for a long time.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Tanya visits an elderly woman once a week.
segments.zh: 坦尼娅每周拜访一位老妇人。当老妇人发现坦尼娅是一名专业裁缝时，她产生了兴趣。坦尼娅给她看了一些自己做的软玩具，老妇人决定自己尝试一下。现在她非常喜欢做玩具，花很多时间制作。也许还达不到坦尼娅的水平，但她从中获得了很大的乐趣。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: When the woman found out that Tanya is a professional dressmaker, she got interested.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Tanya showed her some soft toys she'd made, and the woman decided to try it herself.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: And now she really enjoys it, and spends hours making toys.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: They're not perhaps up to Tanya's standard yet, but she gains a lot of pleasure from doing it.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Alexei is a volunteer with a family that faces a number of difficulties.
segments.zh: 阿列克谢是一个面临诸多困难的家庭的志愿者。通过冷静地与家庭成员讨论可能的解决方案，他帮助他们意识到他们并非无助，他们自己可以做些什么来改善处境。这对他们的自尊心很有好处。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: By calmly talking over possible solutions with family members, he's helping them to realise that they aren't helpless, and that they can do something themselves to improve their situation.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This has been great for their self-esteem.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: And the last volunteer I'll mention, though there are plenty more, is Juba.
segments.zh: 最后我要提的志愿者是朱巴，当然还有很多其他人。她帮助一个患有学习障碍的少女，这个女孩不太擅长和别人交流。朱巴非常耐心地和她一起努力，现在这个女孩在表达自己和理解他人方面都进步了很多。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: She volunteers with a teenage girl with learning difficulties, who wasn't very good at talking to other people.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Juba's worked very patiently with her, and now the girl is far better at expressing herself, and at understanding other people.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: OK, I hope that's given you an idea of what volunteering is all about.
segments.zh: 好了，希望这让你对志愿服务有了一个概念。现在我想……

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Now I'd like ...

segments.speaker: LIZZIE
segments.en: So how are you getting on with your teaching practice at the High School, Joe?
segments.zh: 乔，你在高中的教学实习进展如何？

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: Well I've been put in charge of the school marching band, and it's quite a responsibility.
segments.zh: 嗯，我被安排负责学校的行进乐队，责任挺大的。我想和你聊聊这件事。

segments.speaker: JOE
segments.en: I'd like to talk it over with you.

segments.speaker: LIZZIE
segments.en: I can imagine.

segments.speaker: LIZZIE
segments.en: Yes, hopefully.

items.prompt: Which TWO activities that volunteers do are mentioned?

pairs.p: Volunteers can do all sorts of things
pairs.note: 题干问志愿者做的活动，原文用'do all sorts of things'对应，是概括性替换。
题干'mentioned'对应原文'for example'，表示举例说明。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句在'Volunteers can do all sorts of things'之后，通过'for example'引出具体活动，因此答案在举例部分。

items.prompt: Which TWO activities that volunteers do are mentioned?

pairs.note: 'activities' 被概括为 'all sorts of things'，是上义词替换。
'mentioned' 对应原文中的 'for example'，引出具体活动。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问志愿者做的活动，定位句 'Volunteers can do all sorts of things... for example' 后列举了具体活动，因此答案在此处。

items.prompt: Which TWO ways that volunteers can benefit from volunteering are mentioned?

pairs.note: benefit 和 gain 是同义替换，都表示‘获得好处’。
题干问志愿者受益的方式，原文用‘also gain from it’直接对应。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句‘Volunteers also gain from it’明确提到志愿者受益，后面列举了具体方式，因此答案在此处。

items.prompt: Which TWO ways that volunteers can benefit from volunteering are mentioned?

pairs.note: 题干和原文完全一致，直接定位到讨论志愿者获益的部分。
原文中直接提到简历价值，对应选项E。
原文中直接提到生活目标，对应选项F。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问志愿者获益的两种方式，原文中直接列举了'valuable element of their CV'和'gives them a purpose'，分别对应选项E和F。

items.prompt: What has each of the following volunteers helped someone to do?

pairs.note: 'help someone to do' 在原文中被替换为 'help people'，意思相同。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问每位志愿者帮助某人做什么，定位句提到 'help people'，直接对应。

items.prompt: What has each of the following volunteers helped someone to do?

pairs.note: 题干'helped someone to do'对应原文'encourages them to walk'，表示帮助某人做某事。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到Consuela鼓励他人多走路，与题干'帮助某人做某事'对应，答案A是'walk more'。

items.prompt: What has each of the following volunteers helped someone to do?

pairs.note: 题干问帮助某人做什么，原文用'together they go out'表达一起外出，是帮助的具体行动。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到Minh和年轻人一起外出看电影或访友，对应题干中'helped someone to do'，即帮助某人做某事。

items.prompt: What has each of the following volunteers helped someone to do?

pairs.note: 题干问帮助某人做什么，原文说老人决定自己尝试做玩具，这是帮助的结果。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到Tanya展示玩具后，老人决定自己尝试，对应题干中帮助某人做某事。

items.prompt: What has each of the following volunteers helped someone to do?

pairs.p: helping them to realise that they aren't helpless, and that they can do something themselves to improve their situation
pairs.note: 题干问帮助某人做什么，原文用'helping them to realise...'具体说明帮助的内容。

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干中的'helped someone to do'定位到原文中的'helping them to realise...'，从而找到答案。

items.prompt: What has each of the following volunteers helped someone to do?

pairs.note: 题干问帮助某人做什么，原文用'worked with'表示帮助，且具体描述了结果。
题干和原文都用了'volunteer'，直接对应。

paraphrase.explain: 定位到Juba的例子，原文说Juba与一个有学习困难的女孩一起工作，耐心帮助她，结果女孩在表达和理解他人方面进步很大，对应题干问的'帮助某人做什么'。
